config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa kontrol, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama “kebaikan bersama”. Saya melihat kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban umum, tetapi itu harus dicapai dengan menghormati martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dilindungi, bukan dikorbankan begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter more in some situations, but I do not accept the idea that they should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective in China, collective needs like public order, disaster response, and social stability are very real and sometimes require stronger coordination than in more individualist societies, but that does not justify treating personal rights as disposable. If the social interest always wins, it becomes too easy for authorities or majorities to silence dissent and call it “for the common good.” A better principle is that society can restrict individual rights only when the reason is clear, necessary, and limited, not as a blanket rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan kalau tidak dibatasi bisa berubah jadi pembenaran untuk membungkam kritik atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil. Kepentingan sosial memang penting untuk ketertiban, kesehatan, dan solidaritas, tetapi justru masyarakat yang kuat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi hak warganya, bukan yang menganggap hak itu bisa dinegosiasikan kapan saja. Jadi, bagi saya, kepentingan bersama boleh membatasi hak individu hanya secara terbatas, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank individual rights, because that rule is too easy to abuse. From my perspective in China, I do value collective goals like stability, public safety, and disaster response, and I understand why many people see social order as essential, but I do not think that justifies making individual rights secondary in principle. When “the common good” becomes a vague excuse, it can silence criticism, punish minorities, and leave ordinary people with no real protection. A fair society needs a balance: collective interests can limit rights only when the limit is necessary, transparent, and strictly controlled.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil dan membenarkan keputusan sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan sosial memang penting untuk hal-hal seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan bencana, tetapi menurut saya itu tidak boleh menjadi alasan otomatis untuk mengalahkan hak individu. Justru kalau hak warga dilindungi dengan jelas, masyarakat jadi lebih kuat dan lebih adil, bukan sebaliknya. Jadi, kepentingan bersama boleh membatasi hak hanya kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, and I say that clearly from my own perspective as someone from China. I understand why people here often value stability, coordination, and collective goals, because those things can matter a lot in real life, especially in emergencies or large-scale public policy. But I also think it is dangerous when “society first” becomes a broad slogan that weakens basic protections, because in practice it can silence criticism and leave ordinary people with no real defense. For me, the right balance is that society can limit individual rights only when the limit is necessary, specific, and tightly controlled, not as a general principle that always puts the group above the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
